--- jupyter: python3 author: Corey T. White execute: eval: true freeze: auto date-modified: today format: html: toc: true code-tools: true code-copy: true code-fold: false --- # SSURGO Soil Data in GRASS ::: {#init_session .cell execution_count=2} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Run `g.mapset -c mapset=ssurgo_demo` ended with an error (return code 1): ERROR: There appears to be an active GRASS session in selected mapset ``` ::: ::: Install the `r.in.ssurgo` extension if it is not already installed. This extension is available in the GRASS Addons repository and can be installed using `g.extension`. The source code for this extension is also available in the `tools/r.soildb/r.in.ssurgo` directory of this repository. ::: {.panel-tabset} ## Command Line ```sh g.extension r.in.ssurgo ``` ## Python (grass.tools) ```python tools.g_extension( extension="r.in.ssurgo" ) ``` ::: Set the computational region to the elevation raster and create a relief raster for hillshading. ::: {#set_region .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} region_text = tools.g_region(raster="elevation", flags="p").text print(region_text) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` projection: 1 (UTM) zone: 17 datum: nad83 ellipsoid: grs80 north: 3884960 south: 3876990 west: 272970 east: 280150 nsres: 10 ewres: 10 rows: 797 cols: 718 cells: 572246 ``` ::: ::: Let's also create a relief raster for hillshading or soil maps. ::: {#3ae87d7d .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_relief(input="elevation", output="relief") ``` ::: Import SSURGO data using `r.in.ssurgo`. Note that this will import the soil areas map, hydrologic group map, and ksat maps for low, regular, and high values. The `mukey` column is also imported as a raster with a categorical color scheme applied. The hydrologic group map also has a categorical color scheme applied. ::: {.panel-tabset} ## Local Import ::: {#import_ssurgo_local .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_in_ssurgo( ssurgo_path="../data/gSSURGO_CONUS.zip/gSSURGO_CONUS.gdb", ssurgo_areas="soil_areas", hydgrp="hydgrp", ksat_l="ksat_l", ksat_r="ksat_r", ksat_h="ksat_h", mukey="mukey", hzdept_r=0, hzdepb_r=25, desgnmaster="A", nprocs=30 ) ``` ::: ## SDA Import ::: {#import_ssurgo_sda .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_in_ssurgo( ssurgo_areas="soil_areas_sda", hydgrp="hydgrp_sda", ksat_l="ksat_l_sda", ksat_r="ksat_r_sda", ksat_h="ksat_h_sda", mukey="mukey_sda", hzdept_r=0, hzdepb_r=25, desgnmaster="A", nprocs=30, flags="d", ) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#vector_maps .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} print(tools.g_list(type="vector", mapset=mapset).text) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` soil_areas soil_areas_sda ``` ::: ::: ::: {#cell-soil attributes .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} json_data = tools.v_db_select(map="soil_areas_sda", format="json").json df = pd.DataFrame(json_data["records"]) df.head() ``` ::: {#soil-attributes .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=97} ```{=html}
cat mukey mukey_int compname comppct_r hydgrp drainagecl ksat_l ksat_r ksat_h hsg
0 1 545836 545836 Evard 50 B Well drained 14.400000 32.399999 50.399996 2.0
1 2 545835 545835 Evard 55 B Well drained 50.399996 100.799992 151.199998 2.0
2 3 545835 545835 Evard 55 B Well drained 50.399996 100.799992 151.199998 2.0
3 4 545836 545836 Evard 50 B Well drained 14.400000 32.399999 50.399996 2.0
4 5 545836 545836 Evard 50 B Well drained 14.400000 32.399999 50.399996 2.0
``` ::: ::: ::: {#list_rasters .cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} print(tools.g_list(type="raster", mapset=mapset).text) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` hydgrp hydgrp_sda ksat_h ksat_h_sda ksat_l ksat_l_sda ksat_r ksat_r_sda mukey mukey_sda relief ``` ::: ::: ## MUKEY Map ::: {#cell-mukey .cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code} m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="mukey") # m.d_legend(raster="mukey", title="MUKEY", flags="b") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/mukey-output-1.png){#mukey} ::: ::: ## Ksat Maps ### Regular ::: {#ksat .cell layout-ncol='3' execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="ksat_l") m.d_legend(raster="ksat_l", title="Ksat (mm/hr)", flags="b") m.show() m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="ksat_r") m.d_legend(raster="ksat_r", title="Ksat (mm/hr)", flags="b") m.show() m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="ksat_h") m.d_legend(raster="ksat_h", title="Ksat (mm/hr)", flags="b") m.show() m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="ksat_l_sda") m.d_legend(raster="ksat_l_sda", title="Ksat (mm/hr)", flags="b") m.show() m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="ksat_r_sda") m.d_legend(raster="ksat_r_sda", title="Ksat (mm/hr)", flags="b") m.show() m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="ksat_h_sda") m.d_legend(raster="ksat_h_sda", title="Ksat (mm/hr)", flags="b") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Low](ssurgo_files/figure-html/ksat-output-1.png){#ksat-1} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Regular](ssurgo_files/figure-html/ksat-output-2.png){#ksat-2} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![High](ssurgo_files/figure-html/ksat-output-3.png){#ksat-3} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Low - SDA](ssurgo_files/figure-html/ksat-output-4.png){#ksat-4} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Regular - SDA](ssurgo_files/figure-html/ksat-output-5.png){#ksat-5} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![High - SDA](ssurgo_files/figure-html/ksat-output-6.png){#ksat-6} ::: Ksat maps for low, regular, and high values ::: ## Hydrologic Group ::: {#hydrologic_group_map .cell layout-ncol='2' execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code} m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="hydgrp") m.d_legend(raster="hydgrp", title="Group", flags="bn") m.show() m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="hydgrp_sda") m.d_legend(raster="hydgrp_sda", title="Group", flags="bn") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/hydrologic_group_map-output-1.png){#hydrologic_group_map-1} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/hydrologic_group_map-output-2.png){#hydrologic_group_map-2} ::: ::: Let's compare the hydrologic group maps to make sure they are the same. We can use `r.mapcalc` to create a new raster that is the difference between the two maps. If the maps are the same, then the difference should be all zeros. ::: {#290fca0c .cell execution_count=14} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_mapcalc( expression="hydgrp_diff = hydgrp - hydgrp_sda", ) m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="hydgrp_diff") m.d_legend(raster="hydgrp_diff", title="Difference", flags="b") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/cell-15-output-1.png){} ::: ::: ## Curve Number Install the `r.curvenumber` extension if it is not already installed. This extension is available in the GRASS Addons repository and can be installed using `g.extension`. ```python # | label: install_curvenumber_text # | eval: false tools.g_extension( extension="r.curvenumber", ) ``` ::: {#install_curvenumber .cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.g_extension( extension="r.curvenumber", url="../../../cwhite911/grass-addons/src/raster/r.curvenumber" ) ``` ::: To calculate the curve number, we need both a land cover map and a hydrologic soil group map. The land cover map is from the National Land Cover Database (NLCD) 2024 dataset, which was imported as a COG raster. The `landcover_source` parameter specifies that the land cover map is from the NLCD dataset, which allows the tool to use the appropriate lookup table for curve number values. ::: {#import_landcover .cell execution_count=17} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_import( output="nlcd_2024", input="", extent="region", resolution="region", ) ``` ::: NLCD 2024 land cover map: ::: {#cell-landcover_map .cell execution_count=18} ``` {.python .cell-code} m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="nlcd_2024") # m.d_legend(raster="nlcd_2024", title="NLCD 2024", flags="b") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/landcover_map-output-1.png){#landcover_map} ::: ::: Calculate the hydrolic curve number using the `r.curvenumber` extension, which requires both a land cover map and a hydrologic soil group map. The land cover map is from the National Land Cover Database (NLCD) 2024 dataset, which was imported as a COG raster. The `landcover_source` parameter specifies that the land cover map is from the NLCD dataset, which allows the tool to use the appropriate lookup table for curve number values. ::: {#curvenumber_calc .cell execution_count=19} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_curvenumber( landcover="nlcd_2024", soil="hydgrp_sda", landcover_source="nlcd", output="curvenumber", ) ``` ::: ::: {#cell-curvenumber_map .cell execution_count=20} ``` {.python .cell-code} m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="curvenumber") m.d_legend(raster="curvenumber", title="Curve Number", flags="b") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/curvenumber_map-output-1.png){#curvenumber_map} ::: ::: Let's also run `r.runoff` to calculate runoff depth using the curve number method. This tool requires a precipitation raster, which we can create using `r.mapcalc` with a constant value for precipitation. We need the flow direction and stream maps to run `r.runoff` with time of concentration, which can be calculated using the `r.watershed` tool. ::: {#watershed .cell execution_count=23} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_watershed( elevation="elevation", drainage="flow_direction", accumulation="accumulation", stream="stream", basin="basins", threshold=50000, ) ``` ::: Let calculate the time of concentration using the `r.timeofconcentration` tool, which requires an elevation raster, flow direction raster, and stream raster as inputs. The output is a raster of time of concentration values in hours. ::: {#time_of_concentration .cell execution_count=24} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_timeofconcentration( elevation="elevation", direction="flow_direction", stream="stream", time_concentration="time_concentration", ) ``` ::: ::: {#precipitation .cell execution_count=26} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_mapcalc( expression="rain = 50", # 50 mm of precipitation ) tools.r_runoff( rainfall="rain", direction="flow_direction", duration=24, # hrs curvenumber="curvenumber", time_concentration="time_concentration", runoff_depth="runoff_depth", runoff_volume="runoff_volume", upstream_area="upstream_area", upstream_runoff_depth="upstream_runoff_depth", upstream_runoff_volume="upstream_runoff_volume", time_to_peak="time_to_peak", peak_discharge="peak_discharge" ) ``` ::: Let's visualize the runoff depth and peak discharge maps. ::: {#cell-runoff_map .cell execution_count=27} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_colors(map="runoff_depth", color="water") m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="runoff_depth") m.d_legend(raster="runoff_depth", title="Runoff Depth (mm)", flags="b") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/runoff_map-output-1.png){#runoff_map} ::: ::: ::: {#cell-peak_discharge_map .cell execution_count=28} ``` {.python .cell-code} m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="peak_discharge") m.d_legend(raster="peak_discharge", title="Peak Discharge (m³/s)", flags="b") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/peak_discharge_map-output-1.png){#peak_discharge_map} ::: ::: ## Run model Calcuate the slope and aspect from the elevation raster, which are required inputs for the `r.sim.water` tool. ::: {#r_sim_water .cell execution_count=29} ``` {.python .cell-code} tools.r_slope_aspect(elevation="elevation", dx="dx", dy="dy") tools.r_sim_water(elevation="elevation", depth="depth", discharge="discharge") ``` ::: ::: {#cell-depth_map .cell execution_count=30} ``` {.python .cell-code} m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_rast(map="depth") m.d_shade(shade="relief", color="depth") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/depth_map-output-1.png){#depth_map} ::: ::: ::: {#cell-discharge_map .cell execution_count=31} ``` {.python .cell-code} m = gj.Map(use_region=True) m.d_rast(map="discharge") m.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](ssurgo_files/figure-html/discharge_map-output-1.png){#discharge_map} ::: :::
